The heartbeat/liveness requirements are already implemented in-tree and the targeted heartbeat tests pass, but the required package-wide verification command fails in unrelated `internal/server/perf` coverage. `go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/... -count=1` fails on `TestGraphQLPerfMatrix_Baseline` with `bead(id:) in-process p95=57.28ms > 50ms budget`, which is outside the bead scope and prevents a clean green verification pass. done: - Verified `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` uses `TouchClaimHeartbeat` rather than writing through `Store.Heartbeat`. - Verified `cli/internal/bead/store_test.go` contains `TestStoreHeartbeat_RemovedOrNoTrackerWrite`. - Verified `git grep "Store.Heartbeat|.Heartbeat(candidate.ID)|.Heartbeat(beadID)" cli/internal/agent cli/internal/server` returns no periodic worker-liveness call path. - Ran `go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/... -run 'Heartbeat|Liveness|Watchdog|Claim' -count=1` successfully. blocked: - Required full-package command `go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/... -count=1` is red because of the pre-existing perf baseline failure in `internal/server/perf`. follow_up: - Re-run the package-wide command after the perf baseline is addressed or relaxed in a separate, scoped change.

follow_up:

The claimed-bead liveness path is already externalized in code: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` calls `TouchClaimHeartbeat`, `cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go` stores the lease under an ignored runtime path outside `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, and `git grep "Store.Heartbeat|.Heartbeat(candidate.ID)|.Heartbeat(beadID)" cli/internal/agent cli/internal/server` returns no periodic tracker-writing path. Focused verification passed (`go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/... -run "Heartbeat|Liveness|Watchdog|Claim" -count=1`), but the broader `go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/...` run did not complete in the available window, so I cannot complete the full acceptance gate or justify a code change without further investigation.

{"base_rev":"d091a9e89ff4d1f7a867a513873f2af1ae9a0620","classification":"needs_human","detail":"no_changes","rationale":"The attempt found that the claimed-bead liveness path is already externalized (`TouchClaimHeartbeat` and `cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go`), and no periodic `Store.Heartbeat` tracker-write path exists in the targeted packages. Because the broader `go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/...` gate did not complete, the bead cannot be closed automatically from this attempt alone and likely needs a human scope check.","recommended_action":"review_scope","result_rev":"d091a9e89ff4d1f7a867a513873f2af1ae9a0620","session_id":"eb-f9d6cc6a","status":"no_changes","suggested_amendments":"Update the bead to reflect the current implementation: replace tracker-write remediation with verification/closure criteria, or close the bead if the externalized liveness path is confirmed as satisfying the intent. If keeping the bead open, narrow acceptance to the remaining unresolved verification rather than the already-missing code change.","suggested_followup_beads":[]}

Description supplies current-state file:line citations for every relevant code path (execute_bead_loop.go:884-897, claim_liveness.go:13-25, claim_liveness.go:64-101, store.go:660-685, store_test.go:775-791, execute_bead_loop_liveness_test.go:64-96), a clear proposed fix framed as verification/closeout rather than greenfield work, and an explicit non-scope section. AC is fully numbered with 7 items: AC1-3 each name a specific Test* function, AC4 is a searchable production-code assertion, AC5 provides a -run filter covering all focused tests in one command, AC6 is the full package gate, and AC7 is the lefthook gate — satisfying the 'final two lines are go test + lefthook' requirement. Labels carry phase:2, area:agent, area:beads, kind:bug, spec cross-refs (FEAT-006, FEAT-010), and reliability. Parent (ddx-5cb6e6cd) and dep (ddx-06eb05d8) are explicit, with the dep noted inline as already closed. The description is standalone — no /tmp plan-file citations, no chat references.
